'''''Star Wars: Droids: The Kalarba Adventures''''' is a trade paperback published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] in [[June]] [[1995]]. With an introduction by [[Anthony Daniels]], ''The Kalarba Adventures'' collects the [[Star Wars: Droids Special|''Droids Special'']], the six issues of [[Star Wars: Droids (1994)|''Star Wars: Droids'' (1994)]], the short [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] issue "[[Artoo's Day Out]]", and production sketches from ''Droids''.

==Publisher's summary==
{{Quote|Three movies and countless spin-off activities later, these comic books echo the spirit of Artoo-Detoo and See-Threepio to the letter. Some of the pages are as convincing in character to me, as though I were actually there, playing the part. When a character you have created and loved has been reproduced commercially in every way from bubble bath to bubble gum, you can get a little protective. But with these stories, I know Threepio is safe.|Anthony Daniels|<ref name="Star Wars Droids: The Kalarba Adventures (TPB)">''Star Wars: Droids: The Kalarba Adventures''</ref>}}
==Contents==
*''[[Star Wars: Droids Special]]'' (Welcome to Kalarba)''
*[[Droids (1994) 1|''Droids'' (1994) 1]] (The Greed of Olag Greck)''
*[[Droids (1994) 2|''Droids'' (1994) 2]] (The Indobok Pirates)''
*[[Droids (1994) 3|''Droids'' (1994) 3]] (The Saga of C-3PX)''
*[[Droids (1994) 4|''Droids'' (1994) 4]] (Battle of the B'rknaa)''
*[[Droids (1994) 5|''Droids'' (1994) 5]] (Short Cut)''
*"[[Artoo's Day Out]]"
*[[Droids (1994) 6|''Droids'' (1994) 6]] (Countdown at Hosk)''
